input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project with a failing test — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m` for `"express"`.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. Let me set that up. The project uses .NET 8.0 with MSTest. I can see one test that will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m for "express".

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so the user can iterate on the code while seeing live test results.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-7301acc0162c4f23ba33cc0e0027b1d9 && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch-tests"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but `CalculateShipping("express", ...)` returns `10.00`

The watcher is active — every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, tests will re-run automatically. To fix the failing test, you'd change `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m` on line 23.
